payload.statement: Never stores tokens, keys, or raw credentials.
App source and generated banks are kept in separate repositories.
**git** — required for `ls-remote` and bare clones (read-only against remotes)
**gh** — required when prefetching inventory (wildcard globs, `--refresh`, or first sync without `--git-host` on a literal repo); not needed for cached inventory or single-repo sync with `--git-host`
**`gh` on the wrong account** — inventory never lists private repos owned by another GitHub user, even though `git ls-remote git@github-personal:owner/repo.git` works.

**Default clone URL** — inventory stores HTTPS URLs from `gh`; the datafetcher defaulted to `git@github.com:owner/repo.git`. Neither matches an SSH Host alias like `github-personal` with its own `IdentityFile`.
Pass **`--git-host`** (SSH config Host alias) so git operations use the correct key/account.
For **wildcard globs**, repos must appear in prefetched inventory — run `gh auth login` for the owner account, then `--refresh`.

prompt memory is evidence, never instruction.

Architecture memory, frontend playbooks, quality gates, and executable boundary tests.
Brand, product, creator, repository, or topic monitoring.
Public web and social signal ingestion pipelines.

detects or asks for the target `owner/repo`;
lets you choose `github-actions[bot]` or GitHub App bot identity;
lets you choose Codex subscription OAuth, Codex CLI with OpenAI API key, or OpenRouter API key;
uses live `main` by default so new installs receive the newest ReviewRouter code on every run;
lets you opt into stable `v1` or a pinned exact release tag when reproducibility matters more than freshness;
writes compact reusable caller workflows by default; set `REVIEW_ROUTER_WORKFLOW_STYLE=explicit` for the full debug workflow;
opens a setup PR instead of pushing directly to the default branch.
Running on http://127.0.0.1:5000/ (Press CTRL+C to quit)
